title: Chapter 12: Simple Harmonic Motion - Study Notes
source: A Quick Review of E-PCM for Engineering Entrance - Physics

sections.heading: Conditions and Equation of SHM
sections.content: In SHM the restoring force is proportional to displacement and directed toward mean position. Acceleration is proportional to -x.
specialCases: At x = 0: v is maximum, a = 0.
At x = A: v = 0, |a| is maximum.

sections.heading: Velocity and Acceleration in SHM
sections.content: Velocity and acceleration vary with displacement; their graphs have standard shapes.
formulas: v = dx/dt = A omega cos(omega t + phi)
specialCases: a-x graph is a straight line with slope -omega^2.

sections.heading: Energy in SHM
sections.content: Total energy is constant and split between kinetic and potential energies.
formulas: Total energy E = (1/2) m omega^2 A^2
specialCases: K and U exchange; frequency of K and U is 2f.

sections.heading: Spring-Mass System
sections.content: For a spring-mass system, time period depends on mass and spring constant. For combinations, use equivalent k.
formulas: For two masses on spring: use reduced mass m1 m2/(m1+m2)
If spring cut into n equal parts: k_new = n k
specialCases: In series: period increases; in parallel: period decreases.
If mass of spring is considered: T = 2*pi*sqrt((m + ms/3)/k).

sections.heading: Simple Pendulum and Conical/Torsional Pendulum
sections.content: Pendulum motion is SHM for small angles; effective g changes in non-inertial frames.
formulas: For small theta: sin theta approx theta
In accelerating frame: g_eff modifies period

sections.heading: Damped and Forced Oscillations (Overview)
sections.content: Damping causes exponential decay of amplitude. External periodic force leads to resonance.
formulas: Resonance occurs when driving frequency equals natural frequency.
specialCases: At resonance, amplitude is maximum (limited by damping).
